dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3311 IS: Veterans Affairs Peer Review Neutrality Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Peters (for himself, Mr. Boozman, and Ms. Slotkin) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs

form.official-title: To amend title 38, United States Code, to eliminate conflicts of interest in peer review for quality management of care conducted by the Veterans Health Administration, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Veterans Affairs Peer Review Neutrality Act of 2025.

section.header: Elimination of conflicts of interest in peer review for quality management of care provided by Veterans Health Administration

subsection.text: Subchapter II of chapter 73 of title 38, United States Code, is amended by inserting after section 7311A the following new section:

section.header: Elimination of conflicts of interest in peer review for quality management of care

subsection.text: Each individual responsible for conducting peer review for quality management of care provided by a health care provider at a medical facility of the Department and each member of a peer review committee with respect to quality management of such care shall withdraw from participation in a case review if—

paragraph.text: the individual has direct involvement with the care under review; or
the individual is unable to conduct an objective, impartial, accurate, and informed review.

subsection.header: Neutral assessment of conduct of committee members
subsection.text: Each medical facility of the Department shall develop procedures and guidelines for that facility to require that any initial peer review for quality management that is conducted with respect to care provided by a health care provider who is a member of the peer review committee for that facility be evaluated, discussed, and assigned a final level review by a neutral peer review committee at another facility of the Department.
The table of sections at the beginning of such chapter is amended by inserting after the item relating to section 7311A the following new item:

toc.toc-entry: 7311B. Elimination of conflicts of interest in peer review for quality management of care.
